You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 7f323bef).

## Issue to Resolve

`ij_kotlin_imports_layout` with `|` generates multiple line breaks
## Expected Behavior
I'm currently using the `import-ordering` rule with the following config:
```
ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
```

I'd expect one of my files to be formatted like:
```
package test

import java.nio.file.Files
import java.nio.file.Paths
import java.util.Objects

object TestData {
    fun loadString(dataset: String): String {
        val path = Paths.get(Objects.requireNonNull(TestData::class.java.getResource(dataset)).toURI())
        return String(Files.readAllBytes(path))
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
For some reason (I assume because of the two `|` in the layout config and not having any imports that match `*`), I got two extra line breaks in the file:
```
package test



import java.nio.file.Files
import java.nio.file.Paths
import java.util.Objects

object TestData {
    fun loadString(dataset: String): String {
        val path = Paths.get(Objects.requireNonNull(TestData::class.java.getResource(dataset)).toURI())
        return String(Files.readAllBytes(path))
    }
}
```

It seems that the number of extra line breaks is equals the number of `|` in the config, but if I remove the extra line breaks, I have to run `ktlint -F` twice until it stabilises, which makes the issue even worse.

## Steps to Reproduce
Use the configuration and file above.

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.2
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: (mentioned above)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This seems to be a regression bug in `0.48.2`. It works fine with `0.48.1`.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ImportOrderingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering
 2â†’
 3â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 4â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.resolve.ImportPath
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Sorts the imports according to the order specified in [patterns] + alphabetically.
 8â†’ *
 9â†’ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/a270ee094c4d7b9520e0898a242bb6ce4dfcad7b/idea/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/util/ImportPathComparator.kt#L15
10â†’ */
11â†’internal class ImportSorter(
12â†’    val patterns: List<PatternEntry>,
13â†’) : Comparator<KtImportDirective> {
14â†’    override fun compare(
15â†’        import1: KtImportDirective,
16â†’        import2: KtImportDirective,
17â†’    ): Int {
18â†’        val importPath1 = import1.importPath!!
19â†’        val importPath2 = import2.importPath!!
20â†’
21â†’        return compareValuesBy(
22â†’            importPath1,
23â†’            importPath2,
24â†’            { import -> findImportIndex(import) },
25â†’            { import -> import.toString().replace("`", "") },
26â†’        )
27â†’    }
28â†’
29â†’    fun findImportIndex(path: ImportPath): Int {
30â†’        var bestIndex: Int = -1
31â†’        var bestEntryMatch: PatternEntry? = null
32â†’        var allOtherAliasIndex = -1
33â†’        var allOtherIndex = -1
34â†’
35â†’        for ((index, entry) in patterns.withIndex()) {
36â†’            if (entry == PatternEntry.ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) {
37â†’                allOtherAliasIndex = index
38â†’            }
39â†’            if (entry == PatternEntry.ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) {
40â†’                allOtherIndex = index
41â†’            }
42â†’            if (entry.isBetterMatchForPackageThan(bestEntryMatch, path)) {
43â†’                bestEntryMatch = entry
44â†’                bestIndex = index
45â†’            }
46â†’        }
47â†’
48â†’        if (bestIndex == -1 && path.hasAlias() && allOtherAliasIndex == -1 && allOtherIndex != -1) {
49â†’            // if no layout for alias imports specified, put them among all others
50â†’            bestIndex = allOtherIndex
51â†’        }
52â†’        return bestIndex
53â†’    }
54â†’}

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering
  2â†’
  3â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.resolve.ImportPath
  4â†’
  5â†’/**
  6â†’ * Represents an entry in the imports layout pattern. Contains matching logic for imports.
  7â†’ *
  8â†’ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinPackageEntry.kt#L10
  9â†’ */
 10â†’public class PatternEntry(
 11â†’    packageName: String,
 12â†’    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13â†’    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14â†’) {
 15â†’    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16â†’
 17â†’    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18â†’        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19â†’        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20â†’
 21â†’        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22â†’            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23â†’                return true
 24â†’            }
 25â†’            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26â†’                return true
 27â†’            }
 28â†’        }
 29â†’        return false
 30â†’    }
 31â†’
 32â†’    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33â†’        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34â†’    }
 35â†’
 36â†’    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37â†’        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38â†’        import: ImportPath,
 39â†’    ): Boolean {
 40â†’        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41â†’        if (entry == null) return true
 42â†’
 43â†’        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44â†’        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45â†’        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46â†’        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47â†’
 48â†’        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49â†’
 50â†’        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51â†’    }
 52â†’
 53â†’    override fun toString(): String =
 54â†’        when (this) {
 55â†’            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56â†’            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57â†’            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
 58â†’        }
 59â†’
 60â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 61â†’        if (this === other) return true
 62â†’        if (javaClass != other?.javaClass) return false
 63â†’
 64â†’        other as PatternEntry
 65â†’
 66â†’        if (withSubpackages != other.withSubpackages) return false
 67â†’        if (hasAlias != other.hasAlias) return false
 68â†’        if (packageName != other.packageName) return false
 69â†’
 70â†’        return true
 71â†’    }
 72â†’
 73â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int {
 74â†’        var result = withSubpackages.hashCode()
 75â†’        result = 31 * result + hasAlias.hashCode()
 76â†’        result = 31 * result + packageName.hashCode()
 77â†’        return result
 78â†’    }
 79â†’
 80â†’    internal companion object {
 81â†’        val BLANK_LINE_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 82â†’            PatternEntry(
 83â†’                BLANK_LINE_CHAR,
 84â†’                withSubpackages = true,
 85â†’                hasAlias = false,
 86â†’            )
 87â†’
 88â†’        val ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 89â†’            PatternEntry(
 90â†’                WILDCARD_CHAR,
 91â†’                withSubpackages = true,
 92â†’                hasAlias = false,
 93â†’            )
 94â†’
 95â†’        val ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY: PatternEntry =
 96â†’            PatternEntry(
 97â†’                ALIAS_CHAR,
 98â†’                withSubpackages = true,
 99â†’                hasAlias = true,
100â†’            )
101â†’    }
